claim: RubricLab is an open-source eval harness that uses LLM-as-judge scoring against structured rubrics to catch agent regressions before users encounter them.
actionable: Use RubricLab (github.com/RitikPatill/rubric-lab) to define rubrics, score agent outputs with an LLM judge, and compare runs to detect regressions.
source_title: RubricLab: LLM-as-Judge Scoring for Agent Evals

claim: Run-vs-run diff views show exactly what shifted between evaluation sets across agent versions, enabling precise regression detection.
actionable: Compare evaluation results between versions using run-vs-run diffing to identify which changes caused regressions.
source_title: RubricLab: LLM-as-Judge Scoring for Agent Evals

claim: RubricLab uses a local-first approach where trace files write to disk and the viewer reads them directly without requiring external services.
actionable: Run RubricLab locally without setting up external infrastructure; traces persist to local storage and are accessed directly by the web viewer.
source_title: RubricLab: LLM-as-Judge Scoring for Agent Evals
